summary: Jeff Atwood shares his most frequently used Visual Studio .NET keyboard shortcuts and encourages readers to improve their own keyboard proficiency. He lists essential shortcuts for debugging, navigation, and searching, then points to resources for discovering more shortcuts. He also created a macro that generates a comprehensive keyboard shortcut reference grouped by scope and sorted by key, and provides a diff showing what changed between VS.NET 2003 and 2005.
key_points: Mastering core keyboard shortcuts for debugging (F5, F10, F11) and navigation (F12, Ctrl+minus) significantly improves development workflow
The incremental find shortcut (Ctrl+I) is highlighted as particularly valuable
A custom macro can generate a personalized keyboard shortcut reference that's more useful than published documentation
Comparing keyboard shortcuts between IDE versions helps developers stay current with their tools
money_quotes: I've been trying to improve my use of keyboard shortcuts in Visual Studio .NET.
But enough about me. Which keyboard shortcuts do you use most often?
You can generate a better keyboard reference yourself using my improved keyboard shortcut enumerator macro.
Unless you are a total Visual Studio Ninja I guarantee you'll find at least a few keyboard shortcuts in there that you didn't know about.
key_insight: Investing time in learning and cataloging your IDE's keyboard shortcuts pays dividends in daily productivity, and building your own reference tools can be more valuable than relying on standard documentation.
